_comment_top: Example retention config for VCF 5.2.2. Adjust 'path' values and retention numbers for your environment, then run: python3 vcf_backup_retention.py -c config-vcf52.json --dry-run --verbose. To temporarily disable a target without deleting it, set 'enabled': false.

backup_targets._comment: SDDC Manager file-based backup. Files like 'vcf-backup-<host>-<domain>-YYYY-MM-DD-HH-MM-SS.tar.gz'. Default schedule in VCF is daily at 04:02 + on every state change, so daily count can be high.
backup_targets.name: VCF 5.2.2 - SDDC Manager

backup_targets._comment: NSX-T backups. Layout: <root>/cluster-node-backups/<uuid>-<ip>/<timestamp>/, plus node-backups/ and cluster-backups/. Retention is applied PER node and PER backup type independently. NSX runs hourly cluster backups and daily node backups by default, so volumes can be large.
backup_targets.name: VCF 5.2.2 - NSX-T

backup_targets._comment: vCenter Server file-based backup (VAMI). Each backup is a folder named like 'sn_<ip>M_<version>_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_<base64>='. vCenter has its own retention setting in VAMI, so this is mostly a safety net.
backup_targets.name: VCF 5.2.2 - vCenter Server
